macro_markets.query: What are the most important macroeconomic and stock market news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major index movements, Fed/central bank news, and significant company earnings or announcements.

items.title: S&P 500 Falls for Fifth Straight Week Amid Mideast Tensions
items.summary: The S&P 500 declined 2% last week, marking five consecutive weekly losses for the first time since 2022, as US-Iran ceasefire talks stalled and oil prices surged. US 10-year Treasury yields rose to 4.43% while Brent crude hit $112 per barrel, raising concerns over inflation and central bank policy.

items.title: US-Iran Ceasefire Breakdown Sparks Oil Surge and Stock Selloff
items.summary: US stocks gapped down at the weekly open after weekend US-Iran talks collapsed, with WTI crude rebounding above $100 and surging 8% amid threats of a US naval blockade in the Strait of Hormuz. Prediction markets now see only 20% odds of a deal before April 30.

items.title: Iran Conflict Delivers Third Global Economic Shock, IMF to Downgrade Outlook
items.summary: The escalating Iran conflict is the third major shock after COVID-19 and Ukraine, prompting IMF and World Bank to brace for slower growth and higher inflation at Washington meetings, with emerging markets hit hardest by energy prices. IMF eyes $20-50B in emergency funding, World Bank up to $70B.

items.title: Central Banks Shift Hawkish as Mideast Shock Fuels Inflation Fears
items.summary: Central banks including the Fed face hawkish repricing amid soaring oil prices from Middle East conflict, questioning ability to cut rates or keep up with inflation. Finance leaders at IMF/World Bank Spring Meetings overshadowed by US-Iran talks.

items.title: Upcoming US Data Includes Fed Chair Powell Speech and Key Labor Metrics
items.summary: Key releases in the next 24 hours feature Fed Chair Powell speech at 2:30 PM, Dallas Fed Manufacturing Index, alongside recent economic calendar highlights like ADP Employment Change and ISM Manufacturing PMI amid labor market focus.

crypto_news.query: What are the most significant cryptocurrency and blockchain news from the past 24 hours? Include major price movements, regulatory news, and notable project developments.

items.title: Crypto Market Liquidations Reach $229 Million in 24 Hours, Shorts Hit Hardest
items.summary: The cryptocurrency market experienced $229 million in liquidations over the past 24 hours, with short positions accounting for $142 million and Bitcoin leading at $64.41 million. Over 153,000 traders were affected, including a single $12.41 million loss on BTCUSDT.

items.title: Crypto Market Pulls Back 2.3% Amid Iran Escalation, BTC Drops to $71K
items.summary: The crypto market cap stands at $2.42 trillion, down 2.3% in 24 hours as investors shift to safe-haven assets due to Iran developments, with Bitcoin retreating from $73K to $71K. Aave leads gainers at +8.5%, while IOTA lags at -4%.

items.title: US Spot Bitcoin ETFs See $786M Inflows, Ethereum ETFs Resume at $187M
items.summary: US spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $786.3 million in net inflows, the highest in six weeks, while Ethereum ETFs saw $187.1 million inflows. Analysts note BTC needs to break $76K and ETH $2.4K for trend reversal confirmation.

items.title: CLARITY Act Negotiations Resume in US Senate Amid Stablecoin Pushback
items.summary: The US Senate resumes CLARITY Act negotiations post-Easter recess, targeting markup by late April, with warnings it may stall by May due to elections. ABA pushes back on stablecoin yields, citing misleading safety perceptions.

items.title: BitMine Ethereum Mining Shares Begin Trading on NYSE
items.summary: BitMine, the largest mining company holding Ethereum on its balance sheet, listed shares on the New York Stock Exchange, marking a key milestone as stated by CEO Tom Lee.

ai_news.query: What are the most important artificial intelligence and machine learning news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include new model releases, company announcements, research breakthroughs, and industry trends.

items.title: Anthropic Withholds Claude Mythos AI Model Due to Superhuman Cybersecurity Risks
items.summary: Anthropic has developed Claude Mythos Preview, an advanced AI model with exceptional hacking capabilities that can chain vulnerabilities and write exploit code, deeming it too dangerous for public release. The company is sharing it selectively with major firms like US banks to patch flaws in major OS and browsers before exploitation.

items.title: CBS Analyzes Anthropic's Claude Mythos Warning on AI Hacking Power
items.summary: Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview demonstrates hacking abilities over five times better than prior models, prompting warnings of severe economic, safety, and security fallout if misused. Experts advocate providing it to critical infrastructure defenders to stay ahead of threats.

items.title: Florida Workers Face Longer Hours and Pressure Amid AI Job Fears
items.summary: A Resume.io survey reveals 81% of Florida employees are working extra hours (averaging 2+ weekly) and shortening breaks to prove value against AI, with 67% engaging in 'productivity theater' and over half fearing job loss or role automation.

items.title: Report Claims Everyday AI Tools Exhibit Left-Leaning Bias and Persuasion
items.summary: America First Policy Institute report finds AI systems like ChatGPT and Gemini show consistent center-left ideological bias, potentially shaping user worldviews, with calls for transparency on design choices and safety testing amid examples like Google's senator evaluations.

gaming.query: What are the most notable video game and gaming industry news from the past 24 hours? Include game releases, announcements, esports, and industry developments.

items.title: Rockstar Games Hacked by Shiny Hunters, GTA 6 Data Potentially at Risk
items.summary: Rockstar Games suffered a major data breach on April 11 by hacking group Shiny Hunters, raising concerns over GTA 6's development, marketing, and release amid threats of data leaks and ransom demands.

items.title: Pokemon Gen 10 Leaks: New Pokemon Revealed for Winds & Waves on Switch 2
items.summary: Leaks from Ball Guy reveal a new Pokemon for Generation 10 titles Pokemon Winds and Waves, set for exclusive 2027 release on Nintendo Switch 2 with open-world islands and ocean exploration.

items.title: Amazon's Luna Gaming Service Shuts Down
items.summary: Amazon Luna cloud gaming service announces closure, marking the end of another streaming platform amid industry challenges.

items.title: New Metro Game Teased Amid GTA 6 Hack and Other Industry Updates
items.summary: Rumors of a new Metro game surface alongside reports of GTA 6 hack, buggy Samson launch, disappointing Crimson Desert sales, and poor reception for Starfield on PS5.

tech_news.query: What are the most important technology news and developments from the past 24 hours? Include major product launches, company news, and significant tech industry developments. Exclude AI-specific news.

items.title: Japan Approves Additional $4 Billion for Chipmaker Rapidus
items.summary: Japan has approved an extra $4 billion in funding for Rapidus, a domestic chipmaker, to bolster semiconductor production capabilities amid global competition.

items.title: Wipro Secures $1 Billion Deal from Olam Group and Plans $375 Million Acquisition
items.summary: Wipro has won a $1 billion contract from Olam Group and intends to acquire Mindsprint for $375 million to expand its IT services portfolio.

items.title: MeitY Proposes Including Users and Social Firms in Content-Blocking Hearings
items.summary: India's MeitY is proposing amendments to IT rules to allow users and internet intermediaries to participate in content-blocking hearings, following stakeholder consultations.

items.title: US Judge Blocks Arizona Criminal Case Against Prediction Market Kalshi at CFTC's Request
items.summary: A US judge has halted a criminal case in Arizona against prediction market platform Kalshi following a request from the Commodity Futures Trading Commission.

items.title: NASA and JAXA Test X-59 Supersonic Model in Mach 1.4 Wind Tunnel
items.summary: NASA and JAXA conducted wind tunnel tests on a hand-sized model of the X-59 quiet supersonic aircraft at Mach 1.4 to gather data on high-speed flight.
